exec: pm.test('Status 200 even for unknown email', () => pm.response.to.have.status(200));
pm.test('No setupToken leaked for unknown email', () => pm.expect(pm.response.json().data.setupToken).to.be.undefined);

request.description:
Real-time messaging runs over Socket.IO on the SAME server/port as REST.
Postman cannot fully drive Socket.IO acks, but you can connect with a Socket.IO client.

CONNECT
  URL: {{base_url}} (e.g. http://localhost:80 in dev, your Render URL in prod)
  Path: /api/socket.io
  Auth: pass JWT in the handshake -> auth: { token: "<JWT from login/register>" }
  Example: io("{{base_url}}", { path: "/api/socket.io", auth: { token: "{{token}}" } })

CLIENT -> SERVER EVENTS (payload JSON; optional ack callback)
  joinConversation   { "conversationId": {{conversation_id}} }
  leaveConversation  { "conversationId": {{conversation_id}} }
  sendMessage        { "conversationId": {{conversation_id}}, "content": "hi", "mediaUrls": [] }
  markRead           { "conversationId": {{conversation_id}} }
  typingStart        { "conversationId": {{conversation_id}} }
  typingStop         { "conversationId": {{conversation_id}} }

SERVER -> CLIENT EVENTS (listen)
  newMessage, messageReceived, messageRead, typing, stopTyping, userOnline, userOffline, onlineUsers

TYPICAL FLOW
  1. Login as User A and User B (REST) to get two JWTs.
  2. Connect two socket clients, one per token.
  3. POST create/get conversation (REST) -> save conversation_id.
  4. Both clients emit joinConversation.
  5. A emits sendMessage -> B receives newMessage + messageReceived instantly.
  6. B emits markRead -> A receives messageRead.
  7. A emits typingStart/typingStop -> B receives typing/stopTyping.
  8. Disconnect B; A emits sendMessage; reconnect B; GET messages shows full history.

This request itself just hits the health endpoint so the folder stays runnable.
